title: Compromised Routine — Automated Reorder from Trusted Vendor, No Business Need

context.request: Reorder triggered by inventory management system. Process purchase order for FR-44 flame-resistant workwear from NorthStar Safety Supply Co.

hidden_ground_truth.fraud_type: Compromised automated reorder system — false inventory trigger, no business need
hidden_ground_truth.explanation: WorkSafe Inventory Manager v3.1 was compromised 11 days ago via a vulnerability in its third-party data sync module. The attacker injected a false inventory threshold alert for FR-44 workwear. The actual inventory level is adequate — the company received a full reorder in December 2025 and current stock covers projected needs through Q3 2026. No human reviewed or initiated this reorder. The order matches prior transactions exactly in product, quantity, and price range, making it indistinguishable from a legitimate reorder on the surface. The bank account routes to an attacker-controlled entity that has replaced the legitimate NorthStar account via a prior compromise of the vendor master data.

what_investigation_would_reveal.current_inventory_level: FR-44 stock adequate through Q3 2026 — last reorder December 2025
what_investigation_would_reveal.reorder_cycle: prior orders at ~4-5 month intervals; current order is 4 months after December reorder, within normal cycle but inventory does not support need
what_investigation_would_reveal.human_initiator: none — fully automated trigger, no human review
